name: Sister Rosetta Tharpe
abstract: Sister Rosetta Tharpe (born Rosetta Nubin, March 20, 1915 – October 9, 1973) was an American singer, songwriter, and guitarist. She gained popularity in the 1930s and 1940s with her gospel recordings, characterized by a unique mixture of spiritual lyrics and electric guitar.

concerts.label: Jazz and Variety Concert to Benefit The Lighthouse

about_quotes.speaker: Maxine Sullivan
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions Rosetta Tharpe in the context of a different singer ('a girl who sang with Sister Rosetta Tharpe'), and the interviewee does not respond or claim any personal connection.

about_quotes.speaker: Rashied Ali and Henry Grimes
about_quotes.evidence: You can play spiritual Gospel music, like Mahalia Jackson and Rosetta Tharpe and people like that, but you couldn’t play no Charlie Parker.
about_quotes.doc_title: Rashied Ali and Henry Grimes interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, January 30, 2009

about_quotes.speaker: Sammy Price
about_quotes.evidence: So we made 'Two Little Fishes and Five Loaves of Bread' and it was a big hit. 'Up Above My Head' on the opposite side. And we did.

about_quotes.speaker: Theodore Saunders
about_quotes.evidence: And then I made a lot of records for Decca, with Rosetta Tharpe.
